name: Backblaze B2
description: Backblaze B2 — S3-compatible object storage at $6/TB/month storage, $10/TB egress (free if pulled through the Cloudflare Bandwidth Alliance). Bucket-scoped REST endpoints with AWS SigV4 against s3.{region}.backblazeb2.com. Region examples: us-west-001, us-west-002, us-west-004, us-east-005, eu-central-003. Generate an Application Key at backblaze.com → App Keys; the keyID + applicationKey pair maps to access_key_id + secret_access_key here.

credential_fields.name: region
credential_fields.label: Region
credential_fields.description: B2 region of your bucket — e.g. us-west-001, us-west-002, us-west-004, us-east-005, eu-central-003. Find it in the bucket's endpoint URL in the B2 dashboard.

credential_fields.name: access_key_id
credential_fields.label: B2 Application Key ID
credential_fields.description: Application keyID from backblaze.com → App Keys. Master Application Key works but a bucket-scoped key is safer.

credential_fields.name: secret_access_key
credential_fields.label: B2 Application Key
credential_fields.description: applicationKey returned alongside the keyID at creation. Shown once — regenerate if lost.

tools.name: list_objects
tools.description: List objects in a bucket (S3-compatible ListObjectsV2). Returns XML; parse Contents[].Key / Size / LastModified, plus IsTruncated + NextContinuationToken for pagination.

prefix.description: Filter to keys starting with this prefix.

continuation-token.description: Pagination cursor from a prior IsTruncated response.

tools.name: put_object
tools.description: Upload an object. Pass content as 'body'; the runner sends it verbatim with SigV4-signed integrity hash. B2's S3 API supports objects up to 5GB single-PUT; for larger objects use multipart upload (not yet exposed here).

body.description: Object content. Bytes welcome — the runner sends them verbatim.

tools.name: get_object
tools.description: Download an object. Response body is the raw object bytes (response cap 200MB).

tools.name: delete_object
tools.description: Delete a single object. Returns 204 on success. Note: B2 buckets with file-version retention will keep prior versions — this only deletes the latest.

tools.name: list_buckets
tools.description: List all B2 buckets visible to the application key. Returns XML; ListAllMyBucketsResult.Buckets.Bucket[] has the names + creation dates.
